calendar.matters:
Petition For Settlement Of Accounts And Reports; For Approval Of Attorneys Fees And Trustee Fees
Off Calendar Mar-30-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 291270 The Survivor's Trust of George and Ruth Tamura.

Appearances: Russell Rowen for and with Karen Tamura and Ellis Brenner (Former Trustees). David Tamura in Propria Persona.

The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the amount of fees being requested, the hourly rate, the extent of services provided, the error on title of the Hawaii property, assuming of the mortgage, lack of accounting, the sale of the San Francisco property, etc. Exhibit-A presented by Respondent David Tamura was marked for identification. The Court denied Mr. Rowen's objection to the exhibit not being allowed. The accounting petition on the Marital Trust was taken under submission. The accounting petition on the Survivor's Trust was continued to March 19, 2010 at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Rowen to determine if he is going to request a statement of decision and if so on what issues. Mr. Tamura is to file his response by March 12, 2010 which is to be limited to new issues that have not been previously raised. Mr. Tamura may appear by court call.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Petition For Settlement Of Accounts And Reports; For Approval Of Attorneys Fees And Trustee Fees
Off Calendar Jan-27-2010 Continued To Feb-26-10 At 01:00 P.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 291270 The Survivor's Trust Established Under The George and Ruth Tamura Living Trust.

Appearances: Russell Rowen for Karen Tamura and Ellis Brenner (Petitioners). David Tamura (Beneficiary) in Propria Persona.

Mr. Rowen requested a continuance stating that they had an agreement that the objections would be provided by January 20, 2010, that they were provided on January 21, 2010, that it was 31 pages long and that he needed time to respond. Mr. Tamura stated that the document was sent by Fed Ex on January 18, 2010 and that he was not in favor of a continuance. The matter was continued to February 26, 2010 at 1:00 p.m., in Dept. 204. The Parties will contact the Marin County Court to try and set that pending matter for the same day in order to prevent further traveling costs for Mr. Tamura. If this date does not work, the Examiner is to be notified by February 5, 2010 and the response is also due by February 5, 2010.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Wendy Pun, CSR#12891. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Petition For Settlement Of Accounts And Reports; For Approval Of Attorneys Fees And Trustee Fees
